Title: Heterologous expression of chloroplast-localized HaGGPS confers fast plant growth, early flowering and increased seed yield

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Plastidial GGPP supply node feeding gibberellin, carotenoid and chlorophyll pathways
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses farnesyl diphosphate and isopentenyl diphosphate to form geranylgeranyl diphosphate
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Gibberellins (GA1, GA4; also affects GA20 and GA9 precursors)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes plant growth, cell elongation, flowering and reproductive development
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Apical stems with leaves in tobacco
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for gibberellin biosynthesis; also redistributes flux away from carotenoid branch
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key precursor-supply enzyme; not explicitly established as sole rate-limiting step in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes higher GA accumulation, faster shoot and root growth, earlier flowering, more seed pods and increased seed yield; associated with reduced carotenoid content and unchanged chlorophyll content
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous overexpression in stable transgenic tobacco, Arabidopsis and dandelion; PCR and qRT-PCR; in vitro enzyme assay; GFP subcellular localization; GA quantification; carotenoid/chlorophyll measurements; phenotypic profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Heterologous expression of chloroplast-localized HaGGPS significantly increased GGPS activity in transgenic plants. 2. In tobacco, HaGGPS increased GA1, GA4 and their precursors and caused faster growth, early flowering, more seed pods and ~50% higher seed yield. 3. HaGGPS reduced carotenoid content in tobacco and Arabidopsis, indicating altered flux partitioning from GGPP toward gibberellin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in transgenic tobacco leaves; also expressed in transgenic Arabidopsis and dandelion lines
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse and MS medium growth conditions; constitutive transgene expression
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq in 16-day-old Arabidopsis leaves (Illumina HiSeq2500); accession number NA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target to enhance vegetative biomass, accelerate flowering and increase seed yield in crops; useful for biomass/bioenergy production, but carotenoid reduction should be monitored and potentially balanced by pathway stacking
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced carotenoid content in tobacco (~24%) and Arabidopsis (~28%); broader metabolic and stress-response transcriptome shifts were observed
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unlike direct GA-biosynthetic gene overexpression, HaGGPS increased growth without obvious abnormal petiole elongation or chlorophyll reduction. Growth enhancement was also reproduced in Arabidopsis and Taraxacum brevicorniculatum.
